articles.title: Our naval might confined Pakistan to its ports: Rajnath Singh
articles.description: The Defence Minister said, “It is not just an architectural design or a product of structural craftsmanship, it rekindles a sense of gratitude we hold for our soldiers. The idea is to awaken the zeal for nation building among our youth.”

articles.title: Bhimashankar temple to reopen for devotees from June 10
articles.description: The historic Bhimashankar Temple in Pune district will reopen for devotees from June 10, acting district collector Gajanan Patil announced on Saturday

articles.title: Hezbollah Unleashes Drone Swarm On Israel; Missile & Rocket Barrage Targets Military Sites | Watch
articles.description: Hezbollah has just launched one of its most complex, coordinated drone and missile operations yet. Breaking through months of intense border combat, the Lebanese group unleashed an overwhelming wave of attack drone swarms and missile strikes hitting multiple Israeli military bases at once. Deploying advanced Ababil loitering munitions, Hezbollah claims to have destroyed military transports in Yohmor, smashed the Yaara barracks in northern Israel, and hit a command headquarters in coastal Naqoura. Watch how this surge in unmanned warfare is completely reshaping the northern front.

articles.title: Trump's AI post sparks row as US warns of war in the Middle East
articles.description: Amid rising Mideast tensions, Trump posts an AI military portrait as the US signals war readiness. Iran denies any diplomatic deal, while the conflict between Israel and Lebanon escalates, with Israeli forces pushing deeper into Lebanese territory.

articles.title: Iran-U.S. war could erupt again, warns Washington as Strait of Hormuz blockade continues. What to expect this week?
articles.description: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth, while attending a defense summit in Singapore, said on Saturday that Washington was "more than capable" of restarting the war if necessary.

articles.title: US developing a ‘true friendship’ with Pakistan: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth
articles.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth described ties with Pakistan as a “true friendship”, praised Islamabad’s role in Iran peace efforts and reiterated Washington’s readiness to act if talks fail.

articles.description: Amy Gertner, the wife of Senate hopeful Graham Platner of Maine, had previously alleged that he exchanged sexually explicit texts with other women.

articles.description: Recent moves by the Malaysian authorities point to a more restrictive climate for LGBTQ people in the country, analysts say, and this could be due to political competition as well as the visibility of some events.

articles.description: Given disruptions in supply chains all over the world, the conflict has shown that Iran’s role in the global economy is not merely centred on oil, says this writer.

articles.title: Invisible walls: Caste in early learning spaces
articles.description: An opinion piece examines how caste bias shapes children's experiences in Anganwadis and preschools, from separate utensils to social exclusion. It highlights child rights, equality, nutrition, dignity and inclusive learning.

articles.title: Teachers failing to join SIR duty warned of police action
articles.description: The election office on its part has instructed police stations to take action against erring employees. However, school and college authorities have pointed out the unfairness of this, given the timing of the assignment—the imminent new academic year and some teachers also being called for census duty has them already burdened with work.

articles.title: US-Iran Tensions: War Talk Grows as Deal Hopes Hang in Balance! Read Details
articles.description: Iran is pretty ticked off with Donald Trump's one-sided comments before any deal is even on the table. While Iran's president says they're open to a 'respectable' solution, the US has just banned Iran's authority in the Strait of Hormuz and is threatening to start attacks again.

articles.title: DMK holds meeting to mobilise ‘Gen Z’
articles.description: DMK's 'Gen Z' meeting aims to engage youth in politics ahead of local body elections and potential Assembly polls in Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: Crissy Froyd exposed the NFL’s dirtiest secret after getting fired for speaking out
articles.description: A reporter lost her job after speaking out about alleged relationships between journalists and NFL personnel. She claims several female reporters traded intimacy for stories. Major sports media outlets have not investigated these serious allegations. The reporter's claims highlight ethical concerns in sports journalism where access is crucial. The industry's silence on the matter is notable.

articles.title: Nuclear Risk Rising! Beijing Unleashes Scathing Attack On US Military Expansion
articles.description: The world is facing its most fragile strategic moment since the Cold War. In a powerful address at the Shangri-La Dialogue, Chinese Major General Meng Xiangqing dropped a massive warning to the West, stating that the risk of global nuclear war is no longer remote. Blasting the U.S.-led AUKUS pact for fueling nuclear proliferation and hammering Japan for trying to rewrite its pacifist constitution, Meng warned that a complete lack of dialogue has left a dangerous vacuum. Even more terrifyingly, he cautioned that unchecked military AI could fast turn 'Terminator' scenarios into reality. Watch China’s fierce pushback.

articles.title: ‘Super corruption government’ is coming to exist: Chalavadi Narayanaswamy
articles.description: Chalavadi Narayanaswamy claims a 'super corruption government' is emerging, criticizing ongoing corruption and stagnant development post-dissolution.

articles.title: Coimbatore farmers hold protest seeking full waiver of cooperative crop loans
articles.description: The association had planned to demonstrate in front of the Sulur MLA office but shifted the protest to the Sulur bus stand after police denied permission for the earlier venue. After the protest, members submitted a petition to Sulur MLA N M Sivakumar at his office.

articles.title: Doval's Moscow Mission: India-Russia Defence, Energy & Arctic Cooperation In Focus | Watch
articles.description: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval visited Moscow for a series of high-level engagements with senior Russian officials, reviewing cooperation in defence, energy, maritime connectivity, shipbuilding and space technology. He also addressed the International Security Forum, stressing the need for uninterrupted global trade through critical waterways such as the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea. The visit highlighted growing India-Russia cooperation, including discussions on the Arctic shipping corridor, BRICS coordination and regional security challenges.

articles.title: `Will never bow down`: Abhishek Banerjee, as he gets CID notice, asked to appear on June 1
articles.description: The notice came after a CID team visited his Harish Mukherjee Road property in the morning, only to be told by security personnel that no one had been staying there for the past few days.

articles.title: Arrangements in place ahead of Vice-President’s visit to Kottayam on May 31
articles.description: Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan to inaugurate Deepika's 140th anniversary in Kottayam on May 31, amid tight security measures.

articles.title: TMC Leader Abhishek Banerjee Asked To Appear Before CID Amid Fresh Political Storm
articles.description: TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee has been summoned by the West Bengal CID on June 1 in connection with a forged signature probe involving a letter nominating Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay as Leader of Opposition. Banerjee responded defiantly, accusing multiple agencies of targeting him and refusing to be intimidated. The summons comes amid growing legal scrutiny and follows a recent visit by Kolkata Police personnel to his residence, which authorities described as a routine official exercise.

articles.title: Why would Muslim nations 'bend the knee' to Israel?: Douglas Macgregor
articles.description: Retired US Army Colonel Douglas Macgregor questions why Muslim nations would join the Abraham Accords, calling it akin to 'genuflecting to Israel' and says there is no incentive for any Muslim leader to do anything for Israel today.

articles.title: DK Shivakumar to be sworn in as Karnataka's new Chief Minister on June 3
articles.description: Former Deputy CM DK Shivakumar will be sworn in as the new Chief Minister of Karnataka on June 3. The Congress Legislative Party unanimously elected him as its leader, a decision announced by KC Venugopal after Siddaramaiah proposed his name.

articles.title: The Suvendu Effect- Why Bengal Is Debating A Building Again | Kolkata's Historic Landmark | News18
articles.description: Writers’ Building is back at the centre of Bengal's political conversation.From saffron makeovers to infrastructure decisions, the new administration is reshaping both the look and narrative of governance in West Bengal.Is this merely a change in aesthetics, or a larger shift in Bengal's political identity?As discussions intensify over saffron-themed aesthetics, administrative priorities, and political symbolism, many are asking whether this is simply a makeover—or a sign of a broader ideological and political shift in West Bengal. n18oc_india News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.description: Armed Forces Alert: General Dwivedi vows readiness for Operation Sindoor 2.0 against Pakistan amid evolving multi-domain warfare strategies.

articles.title: Cabinet reshuffle: Can Kerala model help Congress manage two camps as DK Shivakumar takes over?
articles.description: D.K. Shivakumar prepares to take oath as Karnataka CM. Discover how Congress may use he Kerala model to balance cabinet positions and promote younger leaders.

articles.title: Ram Gopal Varma fires back at Ranveer Singh Ban, calls FWICE action a ‘Big Fat Joke’
articles.description: FWICE ban: Ram Gopal Varma has backed Ranveer Singh in the Don 3 dispute, calling FWICE's action a 'big fat joke'. He criticised the federation's role, while Ranveer maintained silence through an official statement on the row.

articles.title: From Mahatma Gandhi to Narendra Modi: India's biggest brand ambassador now is the Prime Minister, says Shekhawat
articles.description: India's global image has transformed, says Union Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat. He added that previously the country's perception was linked to Mahatma Gandhi, but now Prime Minister Narendra Modi is the nation's foremost brand ambassador. This highlights the shift in promoting India as a policy. He believes India's culture and heritage also define its identity. This change in branding is expected to boost foreign tourist arrivals significantly in the coming years.

articles.title: From Mahatma Gandhi to Narendra Modi: India's biggest brand ambassador is now the Prime Minister, says Shekhawat
articles.description: India's global image has transformed, says Union Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat. He added that previously the country's perception was linked to Mahatma Gandhi, but now Prime Minister Narendra Modi is the nation's foremost brand ambassador. This highlights the shift in promoting India as a policy. He believes India's culture and heritage also define its identity. This change in branding is expected to boost foreign tourist arrivals significantly in the coming years.

articles.title: Centre withdraws draft sugarcane order after objections from states, stakeholders
articles.description: The government has withdrawn the draft Sugarcane Control Order, 2026. This decision comes after receiving objections from state governments and other stakeholders. The draft aimed to replace the old order and bring ethanol and khandsari sectors under regulation. Concerns were raised about the impact on small khandsari units and farmers. The withdrawal is seen as a move prioritizing farmer interests.

articles.title: Trump faces major political dilemma over West Asia: Douglas Macgregor
articles.description: Retired US Colonel Douglas Macgregor argues Donald Trump faces a major political dilemma. While Americans would support ending military operations in the Persian Gulf, influential pro-Israel interests could block such a move, he claims.

articles.title: Priyank Kharge dismisses speculation over Yathindra's Rahul meeting
articles.description: Priyank Kharge dismissed speculation over Siddaramaiah's son Yathindra meeting Rahul Gandhi, calling it a common practice. He added he has no demands for the upcoming Karnataka cabinet reconstitution and will abide by the party's decision.

articles.title: BJP leader attacks 'corrupt' Congress govt in Karnataka post CM's exit
articles.description: BJP's Chalavadi Narayanaswamy attacked the Congress govt in Karnataka after CM Siddaramaiah resigned. He claimed the administration is "super corrupt" with "zero development" and a new leader won't change anything for the state.
